Upgrades

Upgrades

Section titled “Upgrades”

Guide for upgrading Evolve nodes and handling version migrations.

Upgrade Types

Section titled “Upgrade Types”

Minor Upgrades (Patch/Minor Version)

Section titled “Minor Upgrades (Patch/Minor Version)”

Non-breaking changes, bug fixes, and minor improvements.

Process:

  1. Stop the node
  2. Replace binary
  3. Restart

Major Upgrades (Breaking Changes)

Section titled “Major Upgrades (Breaking Changes)”

May require state migration or coordinated network upgrade.

Process:

  1. Review changelog for breaking changes
  2. Coordinate upgrade height with network
  3. Stop at designated height
  4. Upgrade binary
  5. Run any migration scripts
  6. Restart

ev-node Upgrades

Section titled “ev-node Upgrades”

Check Current Version

Section titled “Check Current Version”

Upgrade Binary

Section titled “Upgrade Binary”

Using Go:

Using Docker:

From Source:

Configuration Changes

Section titled “Configuration Changes”

After upgrading, check for new or changed configuration options:

  1. Review the changelog
  2. Compare your config with the new defaults
  3. Update configuration as needed

ev-reth Upgrades

Section titled “ev-reth Upgrades”

Version Compatibility

Section titled “Version Compatibility”

ev-reth versions must be compatible with ev-node. Check the compatibility matrix:

ev-nodeev-reth
v1.xv0.x

Upgrade Process

Section titled “Upgrade Process”

Database Migrations

Section titled “Database Migrations”

Some ev-reth upgrades require database migration:

ev-abci Upgrades

Section titled “ev-abci Upgrades”

Cosmos SDK Compatibility

Section titled “Cosmos SDK Compatibility”

ev-abci tracks Cosmos SDK versions. Ensure your app’s SDK version is compatible:

ev-abciCosmos SDK
v1.xv0.50.x

Module Upgrades

Section titled “Module Upgrades”

For Cosmos SDK apps with custom modules:

  1. Update module dependencies in go.mod
  2. Run any module migration handlers
  3. Update genesis if needed

Coordinated Network Upgrades

Section titled “Coordinated Network Upgrades”

For networks with multiple node operators:

Planning

Section titled “Planning”
  1. Announce upgrade timeline (minimum 1 week notice)
  2. Agree on upgrade block height
  3. Share upgrade binary/instructions

Execution

Section titled “Execution”
  1. All nodes stop at designated height
  2. Operators upgrade binaries
  3. Coordinators verify readiness
  4. Network restarts

Handling Stragglers

Section titled “Handling Stragglers”

If some nodes don’t upgrade:

  • They will reject new blocks (if consensus rules changed)
  • They can sync from upgraded nodes after upgrading

Rollback Procedures

Section titled “Rollback Procedures”

If an upgrade causes issues:

ev-node Rollback

Section titled “ev-node Rollback”

ev-reth Rollback

Section titled “ev-reth Rollback”

State Migration

Section titled “State Migration”

Export State

Section titled “Export State”

Before major upgrades, export state:

Migrate State

Section titled “Migrate State”

If state format changes:

Import State

Section titled “Import State”

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

Pre-Upgrade Checklist

Section titled “Pre-Upgrade Checklist”
  • Review changelog for breaking changes
  • Test upgrade on testnet first
  • Backup current state
  • Backup configuration files
  • Notify dependent services
  • Schedule maintenance window

Post-Upgrade Verification

Section titled “Post-Upgrade Verification”
  • Node starts successfully
  • Blocks are being produced/synced
  • RPC endpoints responding
  • Metrics reporting correctly
  • P2P connections established

Automation

Section titled “Automation”

Consider automating upgrades with tools like:

  • Ansible playbooks
  • Kubernetes operators
  • systemd timers for scheduled upgrades

See Also

Section titled “See Also”
  • Troubleshooting Guide - Handling upgrade issues
